execution_summary: Built a Python 3.10-slim Docker image with pandas, numpy, and statsmodels as specified in replication_info.json. Launched the container, reviewed the plan, and upon approval executed the Python translation (Analysis_updated__py.py). The script loaded the replication dataset, applied time and attention filters, created T1/T2 composites, merged into wide format, and ran OLS: Negative.y ~ Realistic.x + Symbolic.x. Execution succeeded without errors. Findings: the Realistic.x coefficient was positive but not statistically significant at alpha=0.05 (p≈0.096), while Symbolic.x was positive and significant (p≈0.006). This aligns with the preregistered focal hypothesis direction but falls short on significance for the realistic threat effect in this replication sample.

code_executed.logs: Model: OLS Negative.y ~ Realistic.x + Symbolic.x; N used = 249. Coefficients: const = 9.144 (p=0.0025), Realistic.x = 1.635 (p=0.0963), Symbolic.x = 1.464 (p=0.0060). Notes: Approximates SEM focal path via observed-variable OLS.
code_executed.environment: Docker container based on python:3.10-slim with pandas>=1.5.0, numpy>=1.23.0, statsmodels>=0.13.5

results.hypothesis_tested: Participants’ perceived realistic COVID-19 threat at Time 1 positively predicts their Time 2 negative affect approximately one week later, controlling for symbolic COVID-19 threat at Time 1.

findings_summary.outcome_name: Coefficient for Realistic.x (T1 realistic threat) predicting Negative.y (T2 negative affect), controlling for Symbolic.x
Coefficient for Symbolic.x (T1 symbolic threat) predicting Negative.y (T2 negative affect)

tables.table_description: OLS coefficients and p-values saved by the script for the focal path model.
Merged T1/T2 wide-format dataset with computed composites.
